source.title: Modification of the active centre of nattokinase to enhance its thermostability using a strategy based on molecular dynamics simulation, steered dynamics simulation, and conservative prediction

gist_memory.paper_problem_memory: Nattokinase has poor thermostability limiting industrial use. Existing modification methods (directed evolution, flexible region design) have not targeted the active center due to high conservation and risk of inactivation. The study proposes a rational design strategy (MSC: MD + SMD + conservation analysis) to identify active center mutations that enhance thermostability while maintaining activity, reducing screening workload and overcoming activity-stability trade-off.

section_gists.heading: 1 Introduction
section_gists.gist: Nattokinase is a fibrinolytic enzyme with poor thermostability. Current engineering methods (directed evolution, rational design) have not modified the active center due to conservation and risk of inactivation. Some reported thermostable mutants (S33T, S62A, Y217K, N218L) are in the active center, suggesting its importance. A new MSC strategy (MD at different temperatures, SMD, conservation analysis) is proposed to systematically identify active center sites affecting thermostability while maintaining activity.
process_signals: Problem: poor thermostability limits applications.
Gap: no active center modification strategy for thermostability.
Hypothesis: active center dynamics influence thermostability; rational design can overcome activity-stability trade-off.
Proposed method: MSC strategy to reduce screening workload and improve prediction accuracy.
methods: Molecular dynamics simulation at different temperatures
failures_or_limits: Active center modification is challenging due to high conservation and risk of inactivation.
Directed evolution leads to high workload and negative results.

section_gists.heading: 2 Materials and methods
section_gists.gist: This section is empty in the provided text.

section_gists.heading: 3 Results and discussions
section_gists.gist: This section is empty in the provided text.

section_gists.heading: 3.1 Mutation strategy design based on MSC method
section_gists.gist: The MSC strategy was applied: (1) MD at 298K and 328K identified 20 residues with ΔRMSF > 0.05 Å in the active center. (2) SMD of substrate docking eliminated 4 residues (L96, L126, G154, M222) that interact with substrate during entry. (3) Conservation analysis excluded strictly conserved residues (score=9), leaving 13 candidates for saturation mutagenesis. Screening by fibrin plate method found thermostability-enhancing mutants at 5 sites: S33T, S62A, A216E/K/R, N218L. Most other mutations caused activity loss. Reported mutants S33T, N218L, Y217K, S62A were also identified, validating the strategy.
process_signals: Step 1: MD at two temperatures to find flexible sites (ΔRMSF > 0.05).
Step 2: SMD to exclude substrate-interacting residues during dynamic entry.
Step 3: Conservation analysis to remove strictly conserved residues.
Result: 13 candidates for saturation mutagenesis.
Screening: most mutations lost activity; 5 sites gave thermostable mutants.
Validation: previously reported mutants (S33T, S62A, N218L) were rediscovered, confirming strategy.
methods: Site-directed saturation mutagenesis with NNK primers
failures_or_limits: Most mutations at 13 candidate sites resulted in loss of activity.

section_gists.heading: 3.2 Purification and characterization of A216E, A216K, and A216R mutants
section_gists.gist: Three A216 mutants (A216E, A216K, A216R) were purified and characterized. All showed increased half-life at 55°C (5.5-, 5.7-, 3.6-fold). pH optimum and stability unchanged despite charge changes. Kinetic analysis: A216K had lower Km (better affinity); A216E and A216R had higher kcat (29.7% and 187% increase). Fibrin plate assay confirmed increased activity and thermostability: A216K had highest activity (507.17 U/ml vs 190.36 WT) and residual activity after 55°C 10 min (76.58% vs 40.19% WT).
process_signals: Purification and characterization of three A216 mutants.
Thermostability: half-life at 55°C increased 3.6-5.7 fold.
pH properties unchanged.
Kinetics: A216K improved substrate affinity; A216E/R improved catalytic efficiency.
Fibrin plate assay confirmed enhanced thrombolytic activity and thermostability.
methods: Fibrin plate assay for thrombolytic activity
failures_or_limits: Fibrin plate method is semi-quantitative and affected by incubation conditions.
Tetrapeptide substrate may not fully represent thrombolytic activity.

section_gists.heading: 3.3 Understanding the mechanism for enhanced thermostability
section_gists.gist: MD simulations at 328K showed: A216E had decreased RMSD and slightly increased hydrogen bonds, suggesting increased rigidity. A216K and A216R had increased RMSD, Rg, SASA but also increased hydrogen bonds and unchanged secondary structure, indicating flexibility without destabilization. A216K had lower potential energy. Local RMSF changes and interaction analysis revealed altered dynamics and new interactions (e.g., salt bridges) stabilizing the structure.
process_signals: MD at 328K to explain thermostability mechanisms.
A216E: rigidification mechanism.
A216K/R: increased flexibility compensated by more hydrogen bonds and stable secondary structure.
A216K had lower potential energy, suggesting more stable state.
Local structural changes and new interactions observed.

section_gists.heading: 3.4 Fermentation process optimisation to enhance the yield of A216K mutants
section_gists.gist: E. coli expression of A216K was optimized sequentially: bottling volume (20%), inoculum size (2%), induction temperature (20°C), pH (8), induction time (20 h), IPTG concentration (0.7 mM). Final yield reached 467.43 U/ml. Single-factor experiments were used.
process_signals: Low expression and inclusion body issues in E. coli.
Sequential optimization of six parameters.
Final yield improved to 467.43 U/ml.
failures_or_limits: Heterologous expression in E. coli suffers from low yield and inclusion bodies.

section_gists.heading: 4 Conclusion
section_gists.gist: The MSC strategy successfully identified A216K with enhanced thermostability and activity. The rational design approach reduced screening workload and overcame activity-stability trade-off. The strategy provides new insights for enzyme engineering.
process_signals: MSC strategy effective for active center modification.
A216K is the best mutant.
Strategy reduces negative results and workload.
Potential for broader application in enzyme engineering.
failures_or_limits: Limited to nattokinase; generalizability not tested.

section_gists.heading: Supplementary material
section_gists.gist: Contains references and supplementary figures/tables. No additional process information.

global_hypotheses_about_storyline: The active center of nattokinase, despite being conserved, contains sites whose flexibility changes at high temperature correlate with thermostability.
A multi-step computational filter (MD ΔRMSF, SMD substrate interaction, conservation) can enrich for mutations that enhance thermostability without abolishing activity.
Mutations at position 216 (A216E/K/R) improve thermostability through different mechanisms: rigidification (E) or increased hydrogen bonding compensating flexibility (K/R).
The MSC strategy can overcome the activity-stability trade-off commonly seen in enzyme engineering.
uncertainties_to_resolve: Why were only A216 mutants chosen for detailed characterization among the five identified sites?
How does the MSC strategy compare to other computational prediction methods in terms of success rate?
Is the enhanced thermostability retained under industrial conditions (e.g., longer times, presence of other proteins)?
What is the structural basis for the activity increase in A216K and A216R?

events.summary: Current thermostability modification strategies for nattokinase do not involve modification of the catalytic centre, despite some thermostable mutants being located there.
Modifying the active centre is challenging due to high conservation, extensive substrate interactions, and high risk of inactivation.
A rational design strategy to enhance thermostability by modifying the active centre can overcome the activity-stability trade-off and reduce screening workload.

A new strategy (MSC) combining molecular dynamics simulation at different temperatures, steered molecular dynamics simulation, and sequence conservative analysis was designed to systematically explore active centre sites affecting thermostability.
methods_or_tools: molecular dynamics simulation at different temperatures

events.summary: The MSC strategy involves: (1) identifying candidate sites with high flexibility changes via RMSF comparison at different temperatures; (2) eliminating sites involved in substrate interactions via SMD; (3) excluding highly conserved sites via sequence conservative analysis.
The MSC method aims to improve screening efficiency and address the activity-stability trade-off compared to traditional directed evolution.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk introduces the problem, hypothesis, and design of the MSC strategy. It contains no experimental results or detailed methods.

events.summary: Thermostability modification studies of nattokinase have not addressed its active centre region, while active centre modifications using directed evolution tend to result in a higher workload and a high percentage of negative results.
A rational design scheme (MSC strategy) is proposed to reduce screening workload and improve prediction accuracy for stability modification of the active centre.
MD simulations at 298K and 328K identified 20 residues with ΔRMSF > 0.05 Å in the active centre.
SMD of substrate docking eliminated 4 residues (L96, L126, G154, M222) that interact with substrate during entry.
Previously reported mutants S33T, N218L, Y217K, S62A were also identified, confirming the strategy's ability to overcome activity-stability trade-off.
Most mutations at the 13 candidate sites resulted in loss of activity.

chunk_events.notes: This chunk describes the MSC strategy design and its application to identify thermostable mutants, including computational steps and experimental validation.

events.summary: Fibrin plate assay measured enzyme activity and thermostability of wild-type rAprY and mutants A216E, A216K, A216R.

MD simulations at 328 K revealed different structural changes: A216E showed decreased RMSD and slightly increased hydrogen bonds, while A216K and A216R showed increased RMSD, Rg, SASA but also increased hydrogen bonds.
RMSF analysis showed reduced flexibility in residues 167–176 for all mutants, and in 209–215 for A216E and A216K, stabilizing loop regions.
Hypothesis that flexible changes in region 167–176 could overcome activity-stability trade-off and serve as a screening indicator.
Structural analysis showed A216E mutant formed additional hydrogen bond with Gln206, enhancing local stability.
Tetrapeptide substrate assay may not accurately simulate thrombolytic activity of nattokinase.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains experimental results (fibrin plate assay, enzymatic properties), MD simulation analysis, and a hypothesis about a stability indicator. Also includes a limitation of the tetrapeptide assay.

events.summary: MD analysis shows A216E and A216K mutants have reduced RMSF around mutation site and more stable secondary structures compared to wild-type AprY.
Structural analysis reveals new hydrogen bonds in A216E (Glu216-Gln206) and A216K (Thr213-His39) that enhance local stability.
A216E mutant shows decreased flexibility in region 126–138, which is correlated with thermostability and may overcome activity-stability trade-off.
Fermentation optimization for A216K mutant in E. coli: sequential single-factor experiments on bottling volume, inoculum size, induction temperature, pH, induction time, IPTG concentration.
Optimal expression of A216K reached 467.43 U/ml under optimized conditions.
MSC strategy successfully identified A216K with enhanced thermostability and activity, reducing screening workload and improving prediction accuracy.

chunk_events.notes: Chunk contains analysis of MD results for A216 mutants, fermentation optimization for A216K, and conclusion validating the MSC strategy.
This chunk contains only reference list entries, no research process events.

evidence_bank.quote: The current thermostability modification strategies for nattokinase do not involve modification of the catalytic centre.

Modifying the active centre of an enzyme is a more challenging process than modifying other regions. This is due to the fact that the amino acid residues in the active centre of an enzyme are highly conserved and interact extensively with the substrate. This makes it more likely that the enzyme will become inactivated following a mutation in this region.

It is therefore evident that a rational design strategy to enhance the thermostability of the enzyme by modifying its active centre represents an effective means of solving the aforementioned problems.

a new strategy based on the combination of molecular dynamics simulation at different temperatures, steered molecular dynamics simulation, and sequence conservative analysis (MSC) was designed in this study.

The initial identification of candidate sites with high flexibility changes was achieved through a comparative analysis of RMSF alterations in the active centre of nattokinase under varying temperature conditions. This was followed by the utilisation of steered molecular dynamics simulation to eliminate sites involved in substrate interactions upon substrate entry into the enzyme's active centre. Subsequently, the highly conserved sites were further excluded through a sequence conservative analysis.

By comparing the results with those obtained from the traditional directed evolution of amino acid residues in the active centre region, this method improves the efficiency of the screening process while addressing the challenge of the activity-stability trade-off in enzyme engineering research.

Thermostability modification studies of nattokinase have not addressed its active centre region, while active centre modifications using directed evolution tend to result in a higher workload and a high percentage of negative results.

it has become imperative to devise a rational design scheme for the stability modification of the active centre of nattokinase that reduces the intensity of screening work while improving the prediction accuracy.

a rational design strategy combining MD-SMD-conservative analysis (MSC) was employed to reduce the screening intensity and improve the prediction accuracy of the active centre of nattokinase. This approach led to the identification of mutant A216K, which exhibited enhanced thermostability and increased activity.

To our expectation, mutations at most of the 13 candidate sites resulted in loss of activity, whereas mutants with enhanced thermostability were found at five sites, 33 (S33T), 62 (S62A), 216 (A216E, A216K, and A216R), and 218 (N218L; Table 2).
This includes mutants such as S33T, S62A, Y217K, and N218L, which confirm the important influence of the active centre of nattokinase on its thermostability (17, 18, 21).

The RMSF results showed that all three mutants showed reduced RMSF in the region of residues 167–176 compared to AprY, At the same time, the flexibility of A216E and A216K in the amino acid residue region 209–215 was reduced.
The results of nattokinase enzyme activity are shown in Figure 3, where wild-type rAprY, A216E, A216K and A216R enzyme activities were 190.

Analysis of the enzyme kinetic parameters of the A216E, A216K, and A216R mutants revealed the underlying mechanisms for the increase in specific enzyme activity.

In order to further confirm the enzymatic properties such as activity and thermostability of the selected mutants, rAprY and three mutants A216E, A216K, and A216R were purified and characterized (Supplementary Figure S4), and their enzymatic properties were detected using the tetrapeptidase substrate method, and the results are shown in Table 3 and Figure 2.
2 Purification and characterization of A216E, A216K, and A216R mutants

In summary, we selected three previously unreported mutants, A216E, A216K and A216R, with both increased activity and thermostability, from the screened positive sites for subsequent characterisation studies.

4 Fermentation process optimisation to enhance the yield of A216K mutants
The results showed that no activity was detected for any of the candidate sites except for P225A, which exhibited high thermostability (Table 2).

In order to investigate the effect of different single-factor conditions on nattokinase production, the enzyme activity of A216K crude enzyme, measured by the fibrin plate method, was employed as an examination index for single-factor optimisation experiments.

event_graph.paper_main_problem: Nattokinase has poor thermostability limiting industrial use. Existing modification methods have not targeted the active center due to high conservation and risk of inactivation. The study proposes a rational design strategy (MSC: MD + SMD + conservation analysis) to identify active center mutations that enhance thermostability while maintaining activity, reducing screening workload and overcoming activity-stability trade-off.

canonical_events.summary: Nattokinase thermostability is poor, and active center modification is challenging due to high conservation and risk of inactivation.
A rational design strategy targeting the active center can overcome the activity-stability trade-off and reduce screening workload.
Design of the MSC strategy: combine MD at different temperatures, SMD, and conservation analysis to identify active center sites affecting thermostability.
MD simulations at 298K and 328K identified 20 active center residues with ΔRMSF > 0.05 Å.
SMD of substrate docking eliminated 4 residues (L96, L126, G154, M222) that interact with substrate during entry.
Conservation analysis excluded strictly conserved residues (score=9), leaving 13 candidates for saturation mutagenesis.
Saturation mutagenesis and fibrin plate screening: most mutations lost activity; thermostable mutants found at 5 sites (S33T, S62A, A216E/K/R, N218L).
Previously reported mutants (S33T, N218L, Y217K, S62A) were rediscovered, validating the MSC strategy's ability to overcome activity-stability trade-off.
Purification and characterization of A216E, A216K, A216R: all showed increased half-life at 55°C (3.6-5.7 fold), unchanged pH properties, and altered kinetics.
MD simulations at 328K revealed mechanisms: A216E rigidification (decreased RMSD, increased H-bonds); A216K/R increased flexibility compensated by more H-bonds and stable secondary structure.
Hypothesis that flexibility changes in region 167-176 could overcome activity-stability trade-off and serve as a screening indicator.
Fermentation optimization for A216K in E. coli: sequential single-factor experiments improved yield to 467.43 U/ml.
Tetrapeptide substrate assay may not accurately simulate thrombolytic activity; fibrin plate method is semi-quantitative.
MSC strategy successfully identified A216K with enhanced thermostability and activity, reducing screening workload and improving prediction accuracy.

13 candidates selected for experimental saturation mutagenesis and screening.
Rediscovery of known mutants validates the computational screening strategy.

MD analysis explains mechanisms of enhanced thermostability.
Observed flexibility changes lead to hypothesis about region 167-176.
Best mutant A216K further optimized for yield.
Limitations of assays noted during characterization.
Overall validation of MSC strategy success.
A216K characterization confirms enhanced thermostability and activity.

must_cover_points: Problem of nattokinase thermostability and challenge of active center modification.
Design of MSC strategy (MD, SMD, conservation analysis).
Computational filtering steps: ΔRMSF > 0.05, SMD substrate interaction, conservation exclusion.
Experimental saturation mutagenesis and screening results (most lose activity, 5 sites positive).
Validation by rediscovery of known mutants.
